frameworks.name: spiking circuit model
frameworks.one_line: spatially ordered E/I spiking network with embedded orientation columnar map

frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: decoding orientation from population activity to quantify information transmission

frameworks.name: parametric population models
frameworks.one_line: tuning and variability structure assumed rather than emergent from circuit

frameworks.name: pattern formation dynamics
frameworks.one_line: inhibition-driven spatial pattern regime that disrupts stable rate activity

core_question: How does the spatial structure of feedforward and recurrent connectivity in a cortical circuit determine population-code performance for an oriented stimulus?
key_assumptions: trial-averaged and trial-variable activity are emergent from circuit structure, not separately stipulated
strong excitatory and inhibitory interactions drive population-wide variability
a columnar orientation map is embedded in the network
manipulations: spatial structure of recurrent (especially inhibitory) connectivity
key_findings: stable-firing-rate wiring regimes transmit all available stimulus information with sufficient decoded neurons
inhibition-induced pattern-forming regimes compromise information flow
network connectivity jointly shapes stimulus tuning and internally generated fluctuations
interpretations: circuit-level connectivity dictates population code performance in nontrivial ways that demand mechanistic modeling
trial-to-trial variability cannot be divorced from the wiring that also sets tuning
